title: fragaria supina
intro: Fragaria supina, also known as the woodland strawberry or low sweet strawberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. It is native to eastern North America and has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, to treat various health conditions. The plant's leaves and roots have been used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion. Fragaria supina is also used in herbal preparations, including salves and creams, to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
benefits: Fragaria supina has many benefits, such as its potential to aid in the prevention and treatment of various health issues, including inflammation, oxidative stress, and even certain types of cancer. Its antioxidants and flavonoids have been shown to improve cardiovascular health, reduce the risk of age-related diseases, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections. Additionally, the plant's anti-inflammatory properties have been found to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory diseases, improving overall quality of life and reducing the need for medication. By incorporating Fragaria supina into one's diet or using its extracts topically, individuals can potentially experience a range of benefits that improve their health, well-being, and overall quality of life.
actions: Fragaria supina has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate joint pain and swelling, making it easier for individuals to engage in daily activities like gardening and playing with grandchildren. Its antioxidant properties also aid in reducing oxidative stress, improving skin health, and promoting healthy aging, allowing people to maintain a youthful appearance and participate in social events with confidence. Additionally, the plant's antimicrobial properties help combat infections, protecting people from illnesses that could disrupt their daily routines, such as work and family obligations. Overall, the therapeutic actions of Fragaria supina contribute to improved overall well-being, enabling individuals to lead more active, engaged, and fulfilling lives.
constituents: Fragaria supina has many active constituents, such as flavonoids (quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin), phenolic acids (gallic acid and ellagic acid), and terpenoids (fragarine and fragaric acid), which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These constituents have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which can help to improve heart health, boost the immune system, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. For example, the flavonoids in Fragaria supina have been found to help lower cholesterol levels and prevent the formation of blood clots, while the phenolic acids have been shown to exhibit anti-cancer properties and protect against neurodegenerative diseases. By consuming Fragaria supina, individuals can potentially reduce their risk of cardiovascular disease, improve their mental clarity, and enhance their overall well-being.
